verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is synthesized as a single-pass type I transmembrane precursor. The membrane-anchored proform mediates juxtacrine signaling before regulated ectodomain shedding (by ADAM10/17) releases the soluble EGF-like ligand that is the dominant circulating form. The TM precursor stage is real and documented on the cell surface, but is transient — the shed soluble ligand is the canonical biological actor driving EGFR/ERBB4 signaling. This matches the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern: surface-accessible at the TM-precursor stage but not a stable canonical surface receptor.
